title: Boat Rental in Chiloe: Costs, Routes and Best Boats for 2026
html:
To explore the mythic archipelago of Chiloe by water, the smartest move is to rent a boat from the main port of Castro, where a half-day skippered tour starts at around EUR 160 for a group of six. BoatHire24 makes comparing verified listings straightforward, so you can focus on the scenery instead of logistics.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Chiloe?

Prices range from EUR 50 per person for a group tour to EUR 800 per day for a crewed motor yacht. Shared excursions from Castro to the nearby islets cost EUR 50-70 per person and last roughly four hours. A private motorboat for half a day (up to six guests) runs from EUR 160. For a full-day catamaran trip that visits the penguin colonies of Isla Puñihuil, budget EUR 400 for up to ten passengers. A crewed Fairline or similar yacht sleeping six will cost around EUR 800 per day.

Where to set off in Chiloe

The main departure point is Castro, with its palafito waterfront and the public pier at Muelle de Castro. Heading south, the fishing port of Quellón gives access to the wilder Golfo Corcovado. On the eastern side, the towns of Dalcahue and Chonchi offer sheltered ramps. For a longer voyage, set out from Ancud and cross the Canal de Chacao towards the mainland. The ferry from Puerto Montt also arrives at Pargua, where you can arrange a transfer to a waiting boat.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Chiloe?

A licence is not required when you hire a skippered boat, but to go bareboat you need an International Certificate of Competence or a valid national licence. Most holidaymakers opt for a skippered charter, and the operator confirms the exact requirements based on your country of origin. The best season runs from December to March, when the prevailing wind is the northerly raco, which can pick up in the afternoon. A local tip: anchor early in the lee of Isla Alao to avoid the chop.

Always check the wind forecast before setting out; the Chilote channels can turn choppy within an hour, especially near the mouth of the Rio Pudeto.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), ideal for exploring the inner estuary around Castro, from EUR 160 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (8-12 guests), best for wildlife trips to Isla Puñihuil, from EUR 400 full-day.
  3. Crewed motor yacht (6-8 guests), comfortable cruising to the outer islands, from EUR 800 full-day.
  4. RIB tender (2-4 guests), nimble for beach-hopping the western coves, from EUR 120 half-day.

How to book a boat in Chiloe

Start by using the boat search to filter by guest capacity and type. Each listing shows photos, the skipper’s experience and the exact price in EUR. You can message the owner direct, and payment is handled securely through the platform. Many operators offer a free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 50 per person (shared) to EUR 800 per day (crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: December, January, February, March
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day) to 8 hours (full-day)
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests depending on the boat
  • Currency: EUR (Euro), most operators list in euros

Questions about boat rental in Chiloe

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Chiloe?

Yes, but only if you hold a recognised International Certificate of Competence or a national powerboat licence, and the owner agrees to the bareboat arrangement.

What is the best month for calm seas in Chiloe?

January and February offer the most settled weather, with average sea temperatures around 15°C and lighter winds in the morning.

How far is it from Castro to Isla Quinchao by boat?

The distance is roughly 12 nautical miles, a 45-minute crossing in a standard motorboat at planning speed.

Can I bring my own food and drink on a charter?

Most skippers allow you to bring your own provisions, and many also offer a simple picnic for an extra fee, check the listing details before booking.

Whether you plan a day trip to the wooden churches or a longer voyage around the archipelago, find a skippered charter that suits your schedule. For a more luxurious option, look at the Fairline for hire, which comes with a knowledgeable local crew. If you own a boat in the region, you can list on BoatHire24 and connect with travellers from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Chiloe and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
